paper_title: Scaling Hard Vertical Surfaces with Compliant Microspine Arrays
source_file: 机理文献/核心文献/08_Scaling hard vertical surfaces with compliant microspine arrays.pdf

bibliographic_record.published_extent: The International Journal of Robotics Research 25(12), 1165-1179
bibliographic_record.version_status: The supplied PDF has 8 pages and no DOI/publication header. The official record is a 15-page 2006 journal article. Evidence locations in this audit apply only to the supplied PDF.

rendering.visual_status: All eight pages were inspected for column order, figures, tables, equations and glyph legibility. Retained crops were inspected after final cropping.

sections_checked: III.B Correlation with Climbing Robot Performance
V SpinyBotII: Climbing with Compliant Microspines
V.A Body Design: Promoting Load Sharing and Stability
V.B Toe and Foot Design: Promoting Spine Attachment and Load Sharing

equations_checked.notes: The exponential density f_X(x)=lambda exp(-lambda x), x>=0, and the stated mean 1/lambda and variance 1/lambda^2 were checked against the rendered page. The paper applies the approximation to both asperity spacing and asperity length.
theta_min = theta_load + arccot(mu) was checked together with the 3.5-8 degree load angle, mu=0.15-0.25 and theta_min=81-86.5 degree text.

sigma_max = Mc/I = 32 f l d/(pi d^4), with proportionality 1/d^2 for constant l/d and force, was visually checked at 400 dpi. The downstream f_max proportional to d^2 interpretation is explicitly conditional on fixed allowable stress.

The Castigliano end-rotation expression, both trigonometric terms, the R^2/(2EI) factor and the stated 1/d^2 scaling condition were visually checked at 400 dpi. Figure 8 is retained to preserve the geometry and sign convention.

p_max=3f/(2 pi a^2)=(6 f E^2/(pi^3 R^2))^(1/3), a=(3 f R/(4E))^(1/3), and 1/R=1/r_s+1/r_a were visually verified. However, the same page prints E=(1-mu_s^2)/E_s+(1-mu_a^2)/E_a, which has compliance units while adjacent equations use E as an effective modulus. The missing reciprocal cannot be silently repaired.

equations_checked.equation: Unnumbered peripheral tensile-stress and f_max equations
equations_checked.retained_as: omitted except for the r^2 conclusion
equations_checked.notes: The PDF clearly prints sigma_T=((1-2 mu_2)p_max)/3 and an f_max expression containing sigma_max and mu_2. mu_2 is not defined and sigma_max is not reconciled with sigma_T or a material failure stress. Exact coefficients were therefore omitted; only the author's conditional square-radius conclusion is retained.

tables_checked.notes: All 12 surface rows, Ra/Rq columns, approach-angle groups, theta_min subcolumns, maximum tip-radius entries and the NaN definition were checked. The evidence card retains ranges and representative interpretation rather than reproducing the full table.

figures_checked.reason: The photograph's key geometry (270 micrometer shaft and 10 micrometer tip) is not needed after the more general model geometry and parameter table are retained.
Irreplaceable swept-volume, traced-surface, approach-angle, tip-radius and usable-normal-region geometry. The final crop preserves every relevant label without page text.
The surface-specific curves are summarized by the retained ranges, finite-scale warning and Table I audit; exact curve digitization would exceed the paper's reported precision.
The monotonic theta_min effect and approximate Gaussian normal-angle distribution are captured textually; no reusable coefficients are reported.

Minimal visual evidence combining the decreasing asperity-density trend, increasing contact-strength trend, surface data and representative usable length-scale ranges. All axes, units and legend are preserved.
Whole-body layout is adequately represented by the contact-polygon, tail and inward-pull descriptions; it is not a paired-claw model.
The photograph qualitatively shows simultaneous engagement but does not expose toe stiffness, travel or individual loads. The independent-toe mechanism is captured more precisely in text.
Required to interpret the curved-beam coordinates, radius, section diameter, force direction, end rotation and deflections used by E3-E4. All labels are preserved.

omitted_sections: General climbing-robot and biological-adhesion background in Sections I-II
Manufacturing-process history not affecting the retained compliance mechanism
Video URL and proposed sensing/speed improvements not needed for the mechanism card

uncertain_items.detail: The supplied source is an 8-page PDF created in 2005 with no journal header or DOI. The official SAGE record is a 2006 journal article spanning pages 1165-1179. It is not safe to assume identical pagination, equations, figures or content.

The printed E definition is a sum of elastic compliances but the adjacent Hertz equations use E as a modulus. This is dimensionally inconsistent unless a reciprocal is missing; the intended correction is not inferred in the evidence card.

uncertain_items.item: Undefined mu_2 and inconsistent sigma notation
uncertain_items.detail: mu_2 is not defined, and sigma_max in the final f_max relation is not reconciled with sigma_T or a material failure threshold. The coefficient is omitted.

uncertain_items.location: PDF p.4-5, Sections III.B and IV
uncertain_items.detail: Surface ordering, contact-strength points and 1-2 N contact capacity are given without sample counts, uncertainty or full curves. They are retained only as trend or order-of-magnitude evidence.

The paper motivates load sharing and shows independent toe mechanisms but does not report toe stiffness, damping, travel, preload or individual-spine force distributions. Uniform sharing is a design objective, not a measured equality.

known_warnings: The source model is two-dimensional and quasi-static; it cannot represent 3D bypass, overhangs, lateral guidance or trajectory competition.
The paper itself reports pseudo-asperity catches, load-induced slip, airborne skipping and alternating static/dynamic friction that are absent from the traced-surface model.
A 65 micrometer laser spot low-pass filters concrete profiles and changes simulated asperity-density ordering.
The 1/r^2 asperity-density and r^2 contact-capacity balance is conditional on a sufficiently small fractal scale and uniform geometric scaling; processed surfaces and sandpaper deviate.
The paper does not support opposed-array preload or paired-claw compatibility and is only B-level for M4.

Poppler emitted missing-display-font warnings for Symbol and ArialUnicode. All equations, tables, figures and retained glyphs were nevertheless inspected on rendered pages; no relevant glyph was unreadable.
character_count_method: Count of CJK Unified Ideographs in the Markdown source. The card uses the allowed extension to 3500 because the source contains separable surface-geometry, friction/search, failure-scaling, compliant-array and system-validation mechanisms.
